note: Generated by `cargo xtask cli-help`. Read this file to understand all CLI commands before updating README.md or writing documentation.

commands.help:
Git-native secrets manager

Usage: gitvault [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  encrypt      Encrypt a file and archive it under .gitvault/store/<env>/ mirroring its source path. For in-place field-level encryption of JSON/YAML/TOML/.env, use 'gitvault seal'
  decrypt      Decrypt a file from the .gitvault/store/<env>/ archive. Accepts either the original source path or the .age store path. For in-place field-level decryption of JSON/YAML/TOML/.env, use 'gitvault unseal'
  materialize  Materialize secrets to root .env
  status       Show repository safety status (gitignore, hooks, recipients, encrypted files)
  init         Interactive onboarding: set up identity, recipients, harden repo, and create config
  harden       Harden repository and optionally import plain files as encrypted secrets
  run          Run a command with secrets injected as environment variables
  allow-prod   Write a timed production allow token to .git/gitvault/.prod-token
  revoke-prod  Revoke the production allow token immediately
  recipient    Manage persistent recipients
  rekey        Re-encrypt all secrets with the current recipients list
  keyring      Manage identity key in OS keyring
  check        Run preflight validation without side effects
  identity     Manage local identity keys
  ai           AI tooling: print skill or context files for Copilot/agent integration
  seal         Encrypt string field values in a JSON/YAML/TOML/.env file in-place (REQ-112)
  unseal       Decrypt all encrypted field values in a JSON/YAML/TOML/.env file in-place (REQ-112)
  edit         Open a sealed file in an editor, then re-seal on save
  get          Get the plaintext value of a single key from a sealed or encrypted file
  set          Set (update or create) a single key's value in a sealed or encrypted file
  help         Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
  -h, --help     Print help
  -V, --version  Print version

Global Options:
      --json
          Output results as JSON
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Select which SSH-agent key to use, by fingerprint or comment (when multiple keys are loaded) [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]
      --identity-stdin
          Read identity key from stdin instead of a file path (pipe-friendly)

CONFIG FILES (override built-in defaults):
  .gitvault/config.toml          project-level (committed with the repo)
  ~/.config/gitvault/config.toml user-global personal defaults

  Sections and keys:
    [env]     default, prod_name, env_file
    [barrier] ttl_secs
    [paths]   recipients_dir
    [materialize] output_filename
    [keyring] service, account
    [hooks]   adapter

  CLI flags and environment variables always take precedence over config files.
  Run `gitvault help` for the full reference or see README.md § Configuration.

Encrypt a file and archive it under .gitvault/store/<env>/ mirroring its source path. For in-place field-level encryption of JSON/YAML/TOML/.env, use 'gitvault seal'

Usage: gitvault encrypt [OPTIONS] <FILE>

Arguments:
  <FILE>  File to encrypt

Options:
  -r, --recipient <PUBKEY>  Recipient age public key (repeat for multi-recipient; defaults to local identity if omitted)
  -e, --env <ENV>           Environment to use (overrides `GITVAULT_ENV` and .git/gitvault/env)
  -h, --help                Print help

Global Options:
      --json
          Output results as JSON
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Select which SSH-agent key to use, by fingerprint or comment (when multiple keys are loaded) [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]
      --identity-stdin
          Read identity key from stdin instead of a file path (pipe-friendly)

Decrypt a file from the .gitvault/store/<env>/ archive. Accepts either the original source path or the .age store path. For in-place field-level decryption of JSON/YAML/TOML/.env, use 'gitvault unseal'

Usage: gitvault decrypt [OPTIONS] <FILE>

Arguments:
  <FILE>  Original source path (e.g. services/auth/config.json) or explicit .age store path

Options:
  -i, --identity <IDENTITY>  Identity key file path [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY=]
  -e, --env <ENV>            Environment for store path resolution (overrides GITVAULT_ENV and .git/gitvault/env)
      --reveal               Print decrypted content to stdout instead of writing to .git/gitvault/plain/
  -h, --help                 Print help

Global Options:
      --json
          Output results as JSON
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Select which SSH-agent key to use, by fingerprint or comment (when multiple keys are loaded) [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]
      --identity-stdin
          Read identity key from stdin instead of a file path (pipe-friendly)

Materialize secrets to root .env.

The files and keys merged into the output can be configured in `.gitvault/config.toml` via `[materialize]` and `[[materialize.rule]]`.

Usage: gitvault materialize [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -e, --env <ENV>
          Environment to use (overrides `GITVAULT_ENV` and .git/gitvault/env)

  -i, --identity <IDENTITY>
          Identity key file path
          
          [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY=]

      --prod
          Require production barrier for prod env

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

Global Options:
      --json
          Output results as JSON

      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts

      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Select which SSH-agent key to use, by fingerprint or comment (when multiple keys are loaded)
          
          [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]

      --identity-stdin
          Read identity key from stdin instead of a file path (pipe-friendly)

Show repository safety status (gitignore, hooks, recipients, encrypted files)

Usage: gitvault status [OPTIONS]

Options:
      --fail-if-dirty  Exit with code 6 if .gitvault/store/ directory has uncommitted changes
  -h, --help           Print help

Global Options:
      --json
          Output results as JSON
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Select which SSH-agent key to use, by fingerprint or comment (when multiple keys are loaded) [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]
      --identity-stdin
          Read identity key from stdin instead of a file path (pipe-friendly)

Interactive onboarding: set up identity, recipients, harden repo, and create config

Usage: gitvault init [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -e, --env <ENV>      Target environment to activate (writes to .git/gitvault/env)
      --output <PATH>  Export the newly created identity key to this file (instead of storing in OS keyring)
  -h, --help           Print help

Global Options:
      --json
          Output results as JSON
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Select which SSH-agent key to use, by fingerprint or comment (when multiple keys are loaded) [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]
      --identity-stdin
          Read identity key from stdin instead of a file path (pipe-friendly)

Harden repository and optionally import plain files as encrypted secrets

Usage: gitvault harden [OPTIONS] [FILES]...

Arguments:
  [FILES]...  Plain text file(s) to encrypt and import (supports globs, e.g. ".env*") If omitted, only repo hardening (gitignore, hooks) is performed

Options:
  -e, --env <ENV>           Target environment for encrypted files (e.g. --env dev)
  -n, --dry-run             Print what would happen without writing any files
      --delete-source       Delete source file after encrypting (default: keep source)
  -r, --recipient <PUBKEY>  Additional recipient keys (age1...) on top of .gitvault/recipients/
  -h, --help                Print help

Global Options:
      --json
          Output results as JSON
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Select which SSH-agent key to use, by fingerprint or comment (when multiple keys are loaded) [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]
      --identity-stdin
          Read identity key from stdin instead of a file path (pipe-friendly)

Run a command with secrets injected as environment variables.

The files and keys merged into the injected environment can be configured in `.gitvault/config.toml` via `[run]` and `[[run.rule]]`.

Usage: gitvault run [OPTIONS] -- <COMMAND>...

Arguments:
  <COMMAND>...
          Command and arguments to run

Options:
  -e, --env <ENV>
          Environment to use

  -i, --identity <IDENTITY>
          Identity key file path
          
          [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY=]

      --prod
          Require production barrier (pass when deploying to the prod environment)

      --clear-env
          Start child with empty environment

      --keep-vars <VARS>
          Comma-separated env vars to pass through when --clear-env is set

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

Global Options:
      --json
          Output results as JSON

      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts

      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Select which SSH-agent key to use, by fingerprint or comment (when multiple keys are loaded)
          
          [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]

      --identity-stdin
          Read identity key from stdin instead of a file path (pipe-friendly)

  -h, --help       Print help

          Disable interactive prompts
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>

      --identity-stdin

Commands:

  remove    Remove a recipient public key
  list      List current recipients
  add-self  Add own public key to the recipients directory
  help      Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

  -h, --help  Print help

Re-encrypt all secrets with the current recipients list

Usage: gitvault rekey [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -i, --identity <IDENTITY>  Identity key file path [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY=]
  -e, --env <ENV>            Only rekey files in the given environment subtree (e.g. --env dev)
  -n, --dry-run              Print what would be rekeyed without writing any files
  -h, --help                 Print help

Global Options:
      --json
          Output results as JSON
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Select which SSH-agent key to use, by fingerprint or comment (when multiple keys are loaded) [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]
      --identity-stdin
          Read identity key from stdin instead of a file path (pipe-friendly)

Manage identity key in OS keyring

Usage: gitvault keyring [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  set                Store identity key in OS keyring
  get                Show public key of stored identity
  delete             Remove stored identity from OS keyring
  set-passphrase     Store the SSH identity file passphrase in the OS keyring; once stored, gitvault retrieves it automatically when loading an encrypted SSH identity
  get-passphrase     Show whether an SSH identity passphrase is stored in the OS keyring (does not print the passphrase value)
  delete-passphrase  Remove the stored SSH identity passphrase from the OS keyring
  help               Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
  -h, --help  Print help

Global Options:
      --json
          Output results as JSON
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Select which SSH-agent key to use, by fingerprint or comment (when multiple keys are loaded) [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]
      --identity-stdin
          Read identity key from stdin instead of a file path (pipe-friendly)

Run preflight validation without side effects

Usage: gitvault check [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -e, --env <ENV>            Environment to validate
  -i, --identity <IDENTITY>  Identity key file path [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY=]
  -H, --skip-history-check   Skip the committed-history plaintext leak scan
  -h, --help                 Print help

Global Options:
      --json
          Output results as JSON
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Select which SSH-agent key to use, by fingerprint or comment (when multiple keys are loaded) [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]
      --identity-stdin
          Read identity key from stdin instead of a file path (pipe-friendly)

Manage local identity keys

Usage: gitvault identity [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  create  Create a new identity key
  pubkey  Print the age public key of the current identity
  help    Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
  -h, --help  Print help

Global Options:
      --json
          Output results as JSON
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Select which SSH-agent key to use, by fingerprint or comment (when multiple keys are loaded) [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]
      --identity-stdin
          Read identity key from stdin instead of a file path (pipe-friendly)

Create a new identity key

Usage: gitvault identity create [OPTIONS]

Options:
      --profile <PROFILE>  Identity profile: classic (age X25519) or hybrid (age X25519 key with a post-quantum-ready annotation, for future migration) [default: classic] [possible values: classic, hybrid]
      --output <PATH>      Export identity to file (optional; default: store in OS keyring)
      --add-recipient      After creating identity, add own public key to .gitvault/recipients/ (equivalent to running `recipient add-self` afterwards)
  -h, --help               Print help

Global Options:
      --json
          Output results as JSON
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Select which SSH-agent key to use, by fingerprint or comment (when multiple keys are loaded) [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]
      --identity-stdin
          Read identity key from stdin instead of a file path (pipe-friendly)

Encrypt string field values in a JSON/YAML/TOML/.env file in-place (REQ-112)

Usage: gitvault seal [OPTIONS] <FILE>

Arguments:
  <FILE>  File to seal (.json, .yaml, .yml, .toml, .env, .env.<suffix>)

Options:
  -r, --recipient <PUBKEY>  Additional recipient age public keys (repeat for multiple)
  -e, --env <ENV>           Environment to use for recipient key resolution
      --fields <FIELDS>     Only seal the listed dot-path fields (comma-separated, e.g. "db.password,api.key")
  -h, --help                Print help

Global Options:
      --json
          Output results as JSON
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Select which SSH-agent key to use, by fingerprint or comment (when multiple keys are loaded) [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]
      --identity-stdin
          Read identity key from stdin instead of a file path (pipe-friendly)

Decrypt all encrypted field values in a JSON/YAML/TOML/.env file in-place (REQ-112)

Usage: gitvault unseal [OPTIONS] <FILE>

Arguments:
  <FILE>  File to unseal

Options:
  -i, --identity <IDENTITY>  Identity key file path [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY=]
      --fields <FIELDS>      Only decrypt the listed fields (comma-separated, e.g. "db.password,api.key")
      --reveal               Print decrypted content to stdout instead of writing back to the file
  -h, --help                 Print help

Global Options:
      --json
          Output results as JSON
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Select which SSH-agent key to use, by fingerprint or comment (when multiple keys are loaded) [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]
      --identity-stdin
          Read identity key from stdin instead of a file path (pipe-friendly)

Open a sealed file in an editor, then re-seal on save

Usage: gitvault edit [OPTIONS] <FILE>

Arguments:
  <FILE>  File to edit

Options:
  -i, --identity <IDENTITY>  Identity key file path [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY=]
  -e, --env <ENV>            Environment for recipient key resolution
      --fields <FIELDS>      Only unseal/re-seal the listed dot-path fields (comma-separated)
      --editor <CMD>         Editor command to use (overrides config and env vars)
  -h, --help                 Print help

Global Options:
      --json
          Output results as JSON
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Select which SSH-agent key to use, by fingerprint or comment (when multiple keys are loaded) [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]
      --identity-stdin
          Read identity key from stdin instead of a file path (pipe-friendly)

Get the plaintext value of a single key from a sealed or encrypted file

Usage: gitvault get [OPTIONS] <FILE> <KEY>

Arguments:
  <FILE>  File to read from (sealed or .age store file)
  <KEY>   Key to read (dot-path for JSON/YAML/TOML, variable name for .env)

Options:
  -i, --identity <IDENTITY>  Identity key file path [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY=]
  -e, --env <ENV>            Environment for store-file path resolution and recipient lookup
  -h, --help                 Print help

Global Options:
      --json
          Output results as JSON
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Select which SSH-agent key to use, by fingerprint or comment (when multiple keys are loaded) [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]
      --identity-stdin
          Read identity key from stdin instead of a file path (pipe-friendly)

Set (update or create) a single key's value in a sealed or encrypted file

Usage: gitvault set [OPTIONS] <FILE> <KEY> [VALUE]

Arguments:
  <FILE>   File to update (sealed or .age store file)
  <KEY>    Key to set (dot-path for JSON/YAML/TOML, variable name for .env)
  [VALUE]  New value (omit to use --stdin)

Options:
      --stdin                Read the new value from stdin (recommended for secrets)
  -i, --identity <IDENTITY>  Identity key file path [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY=]
  -e, --env <ENV>            Environment for store-file path resolution and recipient lookup
  -h, --help                 Print help

Global Options:
      --json
          Output results as JSON
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Select which SSH-agent key to use, by fingerprint or comment (when multiple keys are loaded) [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]
      --identity-stdin
          Read identity key from stdin instead of a file path (pipe-friendly)
